title: Micropera costulata
intro: Micropera costulata is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as a medicinal herb. The plant is reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Micropera costulata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Micropera costulata is used in traditional medicine to prepare herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Micropera costulata is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Micropera, which comprises a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions. Micropera costulata is further classified into the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that include mint, lavender, and snapdragons. Within the family Acanthaceae, Micropera costulata is part of the subfamily Acanthoideae, a diverse group of flowering plants with characteristic inflorescences. The classification of Micropera costulata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
